message.content:
You are diagnosing a real bug for a careful refactor. Tools: Bash, Read, Grep. Work in /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent.

QUESTION: What env flags or tool-registration gate `search_more_evidence`? Search /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/src/polaris_graph/outline/ and the outline-agent tool registration for any PG_* flag or condition that could globally DISABLE or fail to register the search tool. Is search reachable at all in the acceptance config? Quote with file:line.

Actually READ the code (don't guess). Write your findings to /tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/search_diag/global_gating.md (concrete, with file:line quotes + a clear conclusion). Reply ONLY: "global_gating: <one-line conclusion>".

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll investigate the tool registration and env gating for `search_more_evidence`. Let me start by exploring the structure.

input.command: cd /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent && ls -la src/polaris_graph/outline/ 2>/dev/null && echo "---GREP search_more_evidence---" && grep -rn "search_more_evidence" src/ 2>/dev/null | head -50
input.description: List outline dir and find search_more_evidence refs

input.command: cd /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent && echo "===PG_ flags===" && grep -rn "PG_" src/polaris_graph/outline/ 2>/dev/null | head -80
input.description: Find all PG_ flags in outline dir

content.text: The key finding is emerging: `PG_OUTLINE_AGENT` is the master seat (default OFF). Let me read the tool registration block and the seat gate closely.

content.content:
1049	_OUTLINE_ONLY_ACTIONS = ("inspect_basket", "search_more_evidence", "update_outline")
1050	_FINISH_ACTION = "finish_outline"
1051	
1052	
1053	class OutlineAgent:
1054	    """The per-turn decide/execute loop over the outline workspace. Mirrors the SHAPE of
1055	    ``ReactAnalysisAgent._run_react`` (react_agent.py:690) — decide -> execute -> record ->
1056	    check-stop — without inheriting from it (design: WIRING, not building)."""
1057	
1058	    def __init__(
1059	        self,
1060	        workspace: OutlineWorkspace,
1061	        *,
1062	        agent_model: Optional[str] = None,
1063	        max_turns: Optional[int] = None,
1064	        wall_seconds: Optional[int] = None,
1065	        domain: Optional[str] = None,
1066	        retrieval_deadline_monotonic: Optional[float] = None,
1067	    ):
1068	        self.workspace = workspace
1069	        self.agent_model = agent_model or outliner_agent_model()
1070	        self.max_turns = max_turns if max_turns is not None else _max_turns()
1071	        self.wall_seconds = wall_seconds if wall_seconds is not None else _wall_seconds()
1072	        self.domain = domain
1073	        # Iter-2 P1-3 fix: the OUTER while-loop only checked the wall BETWEEN turns, so a
1074	        # single in-flight ``search_more_evidence`` call could itself run well past the wall
1075	        # (observed: 218s overshoot). ``run_live_retrieval`` already has its own internal
1076	        # mid-fetch wall defense (WALL-03, ``retrieval_deadline_monotonic``) but nothing wired
1077	        # it in — the parameter was accepted here and silently stayed ``None`` (unbounded) on
1078	        # every call. Default it from the SAME wall the outer loop uses, anchored at workspace
1079	        # start, so the in-flight fetch/enrich/classify sub-waits inside a single search call
1080	        # are ALSO bounded by the agent's own wall. An explicit override (e.g. a shared
1081	        # pipeline-wide deadline) still wins.
1082	        self.retrieval_deadline_monotonic = (
1083	            retrieval_deadline_monotonic
1084	            if retrieval_deadline_monotonic is not None
1085	            else workspace.started_monotonic + self.wall_seconds
1086	        )
1087	        self.registry: ToolRegistry = self._build_registry()
1088	        if workspace.notebook is None:
1089	            workspace.notebook = AnalysisNotebook(
1090	                query=workspace.research_question, evidence_ids=list(workspace.ev_store.keys()),
1091	            )
1092	
1093	    def _build_registry(self) -> ToolRegistry:
1094	        registry = build_default_registry()  # the 8 analysis tools, byte-identical reuse
1095	
1096	        async def _exec_inspect_basket(evidence_store, data_points, client, **kw):  # noqa: ANN001
1097	            return await _tool_inspect_basket(self.workspace, **kw)
1098	
1099	        async def _exec_search_more_evidence(evidence_store, data_points, client, **kw):  # noqa: ANN001
1100	            return await _tool_search_more_evidence(
1101	                self.workspace, self.agent_model, domain=self.domain,
1102	                retrieval_deadline_monotonic=self.retrieval_deadline_monotonic, **kw,
1103	            )
1104	
1105	        async def _exec_update_outline(evidence_store, data_points, client, **kw):  # noqa: ANN001
1106	            return await _tool_update_outline(self.workspace, **kw)
1107	
1108	        registry.register(ToolDefinition(
1109	            name="inspect_basket",
1110	            description="Inspect a cp3 basket's members, corroboration, and assignment status.",
1111	            requires_data=False, requires_llm=False,
1112	            parameters={"basket_id": "the basket id to inspect"},
1113	            execute=_exec_inspect_basket, tags=["retrieval"], core=True,
1114	        ))
1115	        registry.register(ToolDefinition(
1116	            name="search_more_evidence",
1117	            description=(
1118	                "Fetch MORE real evidence for a section/aspect that is thin or missing "
1119	                "coverage. Derives a scoped query, runs live retrieval, screens junk/off-topic, "
1120	                "and folds surviving rows into the evidence pool."
1121	            ),
1122	            requires_data=False, requires_llm=True,
1123	            parameters={
1124	                "section": "the section title this gap belongs to",
1125	                "aspect": "the specific missing aspect / sub-topic to search for",
1126	            },
1127	            execute=_exec_search_more_evidence, tags=["retrieval"], core=True,
1128	        ))
1129	        registry.register(ToolDefinition(
1130	            name="update_outline",
1131	            description=(
1132	                "Apply validated revision ops (keep/merge/split/retitle/reassign/add) to the "
1133	                "outline draft."
1134	            ),
1135	            requires_data=False, requires_llm=False,
1136	            parameters={"ops": "a revision op list, e.g. {\"ops\": [...]}"},
1137	            execute=_exec_update_outline, tags=["outline_ops"], core=True,
1138	        ))
1139	
1140	        # list_tools meta-tool (redesign 'Scaling the decide step past 20 tools'): the discovery
1141	        # path for non-core tools once the decide menu collapses them into the categorized index.
1142	        # Mirrors how Claude Code defers tools behind ToolSearch — full description + params on demand.
1143	        async def _exec_list_tools(evidence_store, data_points, client, **kw):  # noqa: ANN001
1144	            return _tool_list_tools(registry, **kw)
1145	
1146	        registry.register(ToolDefinition(
1147	            name="list_tools",
1148	            description=(
1149	                "Look up the FULL description + parameters of a tool by name, or list every tool in "
1150	                "a category (retrieval, compute, cross_source, outline_ops). The discovery path for "

1177	        normalized substring-containment or high token-Jaccard match — still IDENTITY
1178	        recognition of an existing section, never an invented one. Returns ``None`` (no guess)
1179	        on no match."""

content.text: Now I have the tool registration. The registration itself is unconditional inside `_build_registry`. The gating is the seat `PG_OUTLINE_AGENT` (default OFF) which decides whether the agent runs at all. Let me check the seam that gates whether `OutlineAgent` is constructed, and whether search is behind any additional condition (VETO at decide-time, or requires_llm gating in the decide menu). Let me check the veto and the decide-menu filtering.

content.text: Now let me read the two registry filter methods to confirm search is never dropped from the menu by requires_llm or a data gate.

content.content:
65	    def register(self, tool: ToolDefinition) -> None:
66	        self._tools[tool.name] = tool
67	
68	    def get_tool(self, name: str) -> ToolDefinition | None:
69	        return self._tools.get(name)
70	
71	    def available_tools(self, has_data: bool) -> list[str]:
72	        """Return names of tools available given current data state."""
73	        return [
74	            name for name, tool in self._tools.items()
75	            if not tool.requires_data or has_data
76	        ]
77	
78	    def _describe_full(self, tool: "ToolDefinition", has_data: bool) -> list[str]:
79	        available = not tool.requires_data or has_data
80	        status = "" if available else " [UNAVAILABLE: needs numeric data first]"
81	        lines = [f"- {tool.name}: {tool.description}{status}"]
82	        if tool.parameters:
83	            for param, desc in tool.parameters.items():
84	                lines.append(f"    {param}: {desc}")
85	        return lines
86	
87	    def get_tool_descriptions(self, has_data: bool) -> str:
88	        """Format tool descriptions for LLM prompt (full listing — every tool + its params)."""
89	        lines: list[str] = []
90	        for name in sorted(self._tools.keys()):
91	            lines.extend(self._describe_full(self._tools[name], has_data))
92	        return "\n".join(lines)
93	
94	    def get_decide_menu(self, has_data: bool, core_threshold: int = 20) -> str:
95	        """The scaled decide-prompt menu (redesign 'Scaling the decide step past 20 tools').
96	
97	        At or below ``core_threshold`` tools this is BYTE-IDENTICAL to ``get_tool_descriptions``
98	        (today's ~13-tool registry prints in full, unchanged). Past the threshold it prints the
99	        CORE tools in full + a one-line-per-tool INDEX of the rest grouped by ``tags`` category, so
100	        the prompt stays compact as the toolkit grows to dozens/hundreds. Call ``list_tools`` to pull
101	        a non-core tool's full description + params on demand. Never elides a CORE tool; never hides
102	        a tool entirely (every non-core tool still appears in the index)."""
103	        if len(self._tools) <= core_threshold:
104	            return self.get_tool_descriptions(has_data)
105	        core_lines: list[str] = []
106	        index: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
107	        for name in sorted(self._tools.keys()):
108	            tool = self._tools[name]
109	            if tool.core:
110	                core_lines.extend(self._describe_full(tool, has_data))
111	                continue
112	            cat = (tool.tags[0] if tool.tags else "other")
113	            avail = "" if (not tool.requires_data or has_data) else " [needs numeric data]"
114	            gist = tool.description.split(". ")[0].split(" — ")[0][:90]
115	            index.setdefault(str(cat), []).append(f"    - {name}: {gist}{avail}")
116	        out = ["CORE tools (use directly):", *core_lines,
117	               "", "MORE tools (call `list_tools` with a name/category for full params):"]
118	        for cat in sorted(index):
119	            out.append(f"  [{cat}]")
120	            out.extend(sorted(index[cat]))
121	        return "\n".join(out)
122	
123	
124	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
125	# Provenance helpers
126	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
127	
128	def _extract_evidence_ids(data_points: list[dict]) -> list[str]:
129	    """Extract unique evidence IDs from a list of data points."""
130	    ids = []
131	    seen = set()
132	    for dp in data_points:
133	        ev_id = dp.get("evidence_id", "")
134	        if ev_id and ev_id not in seen:
135	            ids.append(ev_id)
136	            seen.add(ev_id)
137	    return ids
138
